reason: Confirmed in current code. `audit-persistence.service.ts` lines 141-151 contains an inline comment that explicitly documents the tradeoff: 'SELECT FOR UPDATE on the prior row option forks the chain under READ COMMITTED: lock release re-checks via EvalPlanQual, NOT the result set, so a concurrent insert is missed and two rows chain off the same prevHash. The advisory lock is the minimum-complexity correct alternative; it auto-releases at COMMIT so the window is one short read + one insert.' The serialisation is not incidental — it is the *correct* implementation of the tamper-evident hash chain invariant. All proposed alternatives (eventual consistency, chain-only-sensitive-events) trade away the tamper-evidence property, which is a security property, not a performance one. Furthermore, the inline comment itself satisfies AC#2 ('document as known tradeoff if not fixed'). At local-government scale, 50-200 writes/second is not a practical constraint.

Lines 96-106 and 71-75 confirm that leaves and tasks are already date-windowed, satisfying AC#2 substantially. The finding's only unmet acceptance criterion is AC#1 (hard cap of 200 users), which would silently truncate the planning grid for any organisation with more than 200 staff — a correctness regression worse than the performance concern. This is a performance tradeoff, not an access-control defect: WONT-FIX is the right disposition because the sole fixable AC would break the feature's correctness guarantee.

Verified in current code. audit-persistence.service.ts:154-156 runs `SELECT rowHash FROM audit_logs ORDER BY createdAt DESC, id DESC LIMIT 1` on every audit write. schema.prisma AuditLog model has @@index([entityType, entityId]), @@index([actorId, createdAt]), @@index([schemaVersion]), and a GIN payload index — but NO composite index on (createdAt, id). No migration creates such an index (grep of all migration SQLs confirms). The existing (actorId, createdAt) index cannot serve this query which has no actorId predicate. As the immutable hash-chain table grows this scan cost increases linearly. Prisma 6.19.x PSL does not support sort-direction index definitions, so a raw SQL migration with CONCURRENTLY is required.
fix_sketch: New raw SQL migration: `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY audit_logs_created_at_id_desc_idx ON audit_logs (createdAt DESC, id DESC);`. Use CONCURRENTLY to avoid locking the append-only table in production. Verify with EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT rowHash FROM audit_logs ORDER BY createdAt DESC, id DESC LIMIT 1 — should show Index Scan not Sort+Seq Scan.

reason: Confirmed: no endTime >= startTime ordering validation exists anywhere. The DTOs for tasks (create-task.dto.ts:189-204), events (create-event.dto.ts:55-70), and predefined-tasks (create-predefined-task.dto.ts:64-77) validate only format via @Matches HH:MM regex — no cross-field ordering check. The service layers for tasks and events have no ordering comparison either. Grep for 'endTime.*startTime|cross.*time|order.*time' in dto and service files returns no ordering guards. Migration 20260527140000 dat013 adds only format CHECKs; grep for 'tasks_time_order|events_time_order|predefined_tasks_time_order' returns empty. Adding a DB CHECK would be the FIRST enforcement of this ordering. The triage's medium-confidence caveat is well-founded: since neither the DTO nor service layer has ever enforced ordering, overnight spans (e.g. start=23:00, end=06:00) may exist in production data. Fix is OPEN-FIXABLE but the fix_sketch must account for this — a pre-flight count of rows where endTime < startTime is advisable before applying the constraint, mirroring the SA-DAT-012 pattern.
fix_sketch: New migration (after pre-flight: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE startTime IS NOT NULL AND endTime IS NOT NULL AND endTime < startTime — if zero, safe to add): `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD CONSTRAINT tasks_time_order_ck CHECK (startTime IS NULL OR endTime IS NULL OR endTime >= startTime); ALTER TABLE events ADD CONSTRAINT events_time_order_ck CHECK (startTime IS NULL OR endTime IS NULL OR endTime >= startTime); ALTER TABLE predefined_tasks ADD CONSTRAINT predefined_tasks_time_order_ck CHECK (startTime IS NULL OR endTime IS NULL OR endTime >= startTime);`. If overnight spans are intentional, the business decision to prohibit them must be made explicit before applying this constraint.
